original text: The defining moment Zimbabwean constitutional reform came 2008 after the disputed and violence riddled elections 2008 when the three main political parties entered into transitional Government National Unity and were tasked with the establishment new constitution which was ultimately adopted 2013 following protracted and turbulent process which began 2009. Some segments the civil society however argued that the concerned political parties had ‘captured the constitutional project and narrowed short-term struggle motivated the pursuit party political interests the expense the will the people and nation’s broad long-term interests’, and thereby subverted and/or negated the aspirations the people. against this background that the study therefore assesses the participation, role and significance the rural populace the drafting the 2013 Zimbabwean Constitution. The study traces the history constitutional reform efforts Zimbabwe, beginning with the colonial Lancaster House Constitution 1979, the protracted exercise 2009 2013 which gave birth the current Constitution. focuses the 2009-13 constitution making process case study. The study employs interdisciplinary approach adopting both doctrinal and empirical research approaches. The study employed the doctrinal research approach provide for doctrinal analysis the relevant global, regional and domestic legislation and case law. The empirical research approach, through interviews, was used collect qualitative data from the general members the rural populace and key institutions such political parties and human rights organisations from three selected rural districts, namely Bulilima, Makonde and Mutasa. The study indicated that while significant number the rural populace participated the constitution making process, the legal environment which subsisted during the constitution making process did not allow for the unfettered flow information and ideas, direct result repressive legislation such AIPPA, Criminal Law (Codification and Reform) Act, Interception Communications Act and POSA, among others similar laws, and well the deeply polarised political environment owing the nature the relationship between the ZANU PF-led government and the opposition political parties. The study further showed that the process was heavily dominated the political parties the Global Political Agreement and all the political parties wanted ensure the adoption constitution that best reflected their preferences and partisan views rather than the will the masses, making the 2013 Constitution elitist negotiated document, contrary the provisions Article the GPA which provided for the right Zimbabweans make constitution for themselves and themselves.

coalition promoting initiative expand Medicaid coverage Montana and big increase tobacco taxes help pay for has filed complaint charging its opponents with illegal campaign activities.
The complaint was filed Monday with the state Commissioner Political Practices Tara Veazey, initiative campaign manager for Healthy Montana, coalition health-care organizations and other groups that pushing Initiative 185, which would raise the state tax cigarettes pack.
Those activities include the failure report expenditures made the campaign against I-185, and the use automated telephone solicitations, commonly known “robocalls,” which are illegal Montana unless live operator first obtains permission deliver the recorded message.
The charges are aimed Montanans Against Tax Hikes, group based Helena, Decision Point Consulting, based Ohio, “and any other now unknown entity committee” that has financially supported the illegal activities.
Commissioner Political Practices Jeff Mangan said the complaint was filed Monday, but still has examined determine whether meets all the requirements accepted his office. said that decision should made Wednesday.
Veasey said interview Monday that the complaint won’t have any bearing I-185, which will appear the ballot November supporters manage collect verified signatures from least 25,468 registered voters statewide June 22.
She said the complaint was filed force dark-money groups publicly revealed, and let Montanans know who spending money influence elections.
“We just want make sure that’s being disclosed, early and often possible,” she said.
Besides raising state taxes cigarettes pack, I-185 would increase taxes smokeless tobacco percent and create new taxes vaping and products.
also would extend Montana’s expanded Medicaid program, which provides government-funded health care more than 94,000 low-income Montanans, past 2019.
Denowh, the former director the Montana Republican Party and president the Montana Group, political consulting firm based Helena, did not return calls seeking comment, but told Yellowstone Public Radio was still reviewing the complaint and had comment.

That C-6 report, fact, lists only two things: donations totaling $325 and debts $1,700.
“The failure opponents I-185 file any the required financial reports disclose any information about their income sources expenditures represents clear disregard for Montana’s reporting requirements and warrants full and immediate investigation,” the complaint says.

The 2018 Berlin anti-semitic attack was attack street the German capital Berlin. Two young men wearing Jewish skullcaps were insulted Arabic-speaking passers-by. One the two, Israeli citizen, was beaten with belt Palestinian attacker. Video footage led public outrage.
There have been other similar incidents involving Muslim assailants particular. spokesman for the Jewish community said the attack showed that "Jewish people are not safe here".
The number overall anti-Semitic offenses Berlin has been rising recent years. 2017, police had registered 288 anti-Semitic acts; 2016, 197 anti-Semitic cases had been recorded. The Research and Information Center Antisemitism Berlin (RIAS) counts total 947 anti-Semitic incidents for 2017, but there may unrecorded cases.
April 2018, Adam Armoush, 21-year-old Israeli Arab living Berlin and his German friend Moroccan origin went through street Prenzlauer Berg taking selfies while wearing kippahs. According Armoush, was trying win argument with Jewish friends his Israel proving that could walk through Berlin wearing Jewish skullcap. one point, argument with three Arab-speaking young men started, which was followed violent attack. Armoush managed document the violence with his cell phone and later allowed the video shared Facebook, from where the video went viral. The video shows the main attacker, later identified 19-year-old Palestinian from refugee camp Syria who had come Germany war refugee, beat Armoush repeatedly with his belt. During the onslaught, the attacker shouted the word "Yahudi", the Arabic word for Jew, before one his companions intervened and dragged him away. According Armoush, the main attacker had also tried hurt him with glass bottle. consequence the beating, Armoush suffered minor injuries. Before turning himself police, the main attacker contacted representative the Palestinian student community Berlin and appeared short video that was distributed social media. They denied was hostile towards Jews that his actions had been motivated anti-Semitism.
The main attacker turned himself accompanied his lawyer the day after the incident. arrest warrant against him was executed.
Several politicians, among them chancellor Angela Merkel and foreign minister Heiko Maas condemned the attack. the Israeli television, Merkel warned persistent hatred Jews Germany. She spoke "different form anti-Semitism" coming from people Arab descent. After this and other incidents, the President the Central Council Jews Germany, Josef Schuster, warned Jewish men "not show themselves openly with kippah the metropolitan milieu Germany".
Under the slogan "Berlin Wears Kippah" the Jewish community Berlin called all citizens wear the kippah April solidarity with the victims.
"Anti-Semitic crimes increase dramatically Germany". Jewish Telegraphic Agency. August 2018. Retrieved August 2018.
Eddy, Melissa; Curry, Andrew (18 April 2018). "Fury Germany Rap Duo With Anti-Jewish Lyrics Gets Award". New York Times. Retrieved April 2018.
"'Jewish Man' Attacked Berlin Admits He's Israeli-Arab Who Didn't Believe Germany Was anti-Semitic". Haaretz. DPA, AP. April 2018. Retrieved April 2018.

you're overweight tech, like am, though, you would smiling happily.
I'll run through few charts and you'll see why. Let's start looking shipments for the tech sector whole and than for the sub-sectors computers, semiconductors and communications equipment.
With the great improvement shipments, it's disappointing see Tech lagging other sectors like Materials and Consumer Discretionary. Some would argue, however, that shipments are the past, and stocks move based future performance.
that's truly the case, Tech might still the place be. Take look these charts showing new orders.
You can see that new orders are quite strong, again reaching levels last seen 2008. Though communication equipment lagging the other sub-sectors, too shows increasing orders.
have written several posts (here, here and here) how the tech earnings scorecard this earnings season has been pretty darn strong. have contended that tech stocks should getting more investor interest. HP, which the abbreviation Hewlett-Packard, one the world’s major companies. headquartered Palo Alto, California. widely known for variety products encompassing both hardware and software.
HP, company has rich history computer technology. Although the company born 1939, wasn’t until much later the 60s that the company actually started delve into manufacturing minicomputers. Laptops came lot later and the first was the HP-110 1984. More products followed this lineup after this.
HP, present, offers two major laptop series. These are the Pavilion and Envy series notebooks. The Pavilion lineup was introduced 2004, with the Envy lineup coming almost years later.
When comes India, does have huge presence the country. The company easily ranks within the top five brands that are available India. There huge network retail stores and even company showrooms where products are available. Even popular ecommerce platforms offer discounts and offers for laptops.
Most students and professionals prefer HP, mainly because its reputation brand and also the quality products offered. Many the laptops models are also reasonably priced, which means that they are largely affordable many buyers.
offers some the best laptops models and their design really stands out among the crowd. The Envy series laptops are more elegant than the Pavilion series. This also because they are tad bit expensive.

original text:
Parades with floats, contests determine the fastest biggest best, and enough warmth and hospitality charm the “uptight” out just about anyone, the small-town festivals South Texas have provided decades great food, live music, and tried-and-true traditions for both locals and visitors. Just bring your smile and stay while!
Head down country road sunny day the South Texas Plains, and it’s good bet you’ll find small town celebrating something, whether historical date or, many these farming communities, top crop commodity.
few these small-town festivals have been happening for long—we’re talking decades—and have grown popular that they’ve hit the big time, drawing record crowds from around the state, the country, and some cases, the world.
With family San Antonio, often plan visits coincide with these annual events. Each one easy day trip from the Alamo City, and everyone welcome.
The first event 1948 was created the Rotary Club give WWII veterans incentive return family farms and help improve the area’s berries. Seems they did great job, because today the Poteet festival draws more than 100,000 visitors during the festival weekend (usually mid-April).
took the 45-minute drive south and jumped into the Strawberry Fest with both feet. Arriving good hour before lunch, indulged some appetizers, including bowl fresh strawberries, strawberry lemonade, and slice strawberry nut bread. Sufficiently sated (for the moment), dropped the Strawberry Judging, where size, color, and sweetness were the elements success.
That afternoon, nibbled BBQ, Tex-Mex, and various fried items (Oreos, pickles, and Frito pies) and still managed make our way over the rodeo arena marvel team roping and bull riding. The evening ended with amazing fireworks display and, most notably, brownie.
One the oldest watermelon festivals the state, the Jubilee transforms the city Stockdale into watermelon wonderland every June. headed down Saturday bit shy hour’s drive from San Antonio) and were just time watch the winners cross the finish line the Watermelon Crawl before finding spot watch the parade.
Big and boisterous, the Jubilee Parade had every form wheeled transportation imaginable, along with watermelon-themed floats, crazy costumes, marching bands, and more. Special acclaim was reserved for the Jubilee royalty—the newly crowned “Queen Melone,” Miss Rodeo Stockdale, and their respective courts.
favorite event was the Jubilee Dog Show and Races. Costumed pets were judged for their watermelon-themed outfits, and then was off the races, with owners cheering their four-pawed competitors on.
When was first discussed 1938, was going called the “Peanut Pow Wow.” The more subtle “Peanut Festival” won out, however, and that’s how the town got its “goober” on.
Just minutes south San Antonio, Floresville pretty place with stunning historic courthouse the town square. here that the Peanut Festival festivities take place each year. When arrived, families were enjoying the carnival rides, which skipped favor going nuts the food booths. Peanut butter treats, peanut brittle, and peanut cookies were all peanut perfection.
The parade featured the new Queen Tuneap and King Reboog (“peanut” and “goober” spelled backward). The queen’s coronation gown was extraordinary, with elaborately hand-sewn details decorating train that was several yards long. Afterward, went watch the famous washer tournament, and ended the day two-stepping live country music the outdoor street dance.
The world famous Texas 1015 sweet onion was developed Weslaco, located near the state’s southern tip. The 1015 comes from the suggested planting date, October 15, but the festival held late March before farmers have buckle down for their bumper crops June.
were down South Padre Island for spring break this year, decided roll over Weslaco experience just how sweet the sweet onion festival could be. With all the onion-themed events, live music, and small town hospitality, had great time.
The big draw was the onion eating contest. All ages are welcome enter, but since involved actually biting into raw onion, chose cheer the brave competitors from the sidelines.
The onion recipe contest, however, was another matter. Each recipe had incorporate least half cup onions, and were eager get the recipes for winning dishes like onion, shrimp and pork dumplings, chocolate covered onion rings, and from the dessert category, butter-poached baby onion tart and lemon-thyme cake.
Drive minutes west San Antonio Highway and you’ll find the South Texas Maize, celebration harvest time the countryside. Open September November 15, the centerpiece seven-acre maze planted with sorghum that produces stalks 12-feet high. Finding your way through the “maize” depends how you answer the questions strategic intersections along the way. Other attractions the site include Paw Paw’s Pig Races, the Hay Bale Jump, and Farmer Ken’s Kiddie Korral.
